creator: zyj20@cl.cam.ac.uk
text:
Created attachment 256235
leak_expl.py

- Overview:
A kernel out-of-bounds read can be triggered within a jail that allows raw sockets, leading to kernel information disclosure. IPv6 fragment(s) that reassembles to an IPv6 packet containing a hop-by-hop header as its first extension header bypasses the sanitisation checks in ip6_input_hbh called by ip6_input.

- Root cause:
The specific scenario to trigger the bug is when the system has a raw socket open for IPv6 hop-by-hop packets and also has the socket option of receiving hop-by-hop options as such (requires superuser/jail):

int fd = socket(AF_INET6, SOCK_RAW, IPPROTO_HOPOPTS);
int optval = 1;
setsockopt(fd, IPPROTO_IPV6, IPV6_RECVHOPOPTS, &optval, sizeof(optval);

Then, ip6_savecontrol would be called with a hop-by-hop extension header of any length, causing an out-of-bounds read off the mbuf buffer holding the reassembled IPv6 packet.

- Steps to reproduce:
Please see attached a PoC.

┌─[zyj20@morello-pwnbox] - [~/vms] - [Sat Dec 28, 17:42]
└─[$]> sudo python ~/leak_expl.py 2001:db8::1235
Password:
.
Sent 1 packets.

===

root@freebsd-aarch64:~ # ./leak_recv 0
leak start: 
0x30100b53a
0xe1ff0080
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de
0xdeadc0dedeadc0de

- Kernel commit and config:
This is reproducible as of the latest commit on 28th December 2024 in a GENERIC kernel.
